diagrams.description: Social Work is a practice‑based profession and academic discipline that promotes social change, empowerment, and the well‑being of individuals and communities. This flowchart outlines its systematic logic: the ultimate goal is to enhance human well‑being and meet basic needs, with particular attention to the vulnerable and oppressed. It integrates core foundational concepts (person‑in‑environment, social justice, human rights, empowerment, intersectionality) with major theoretical frameworks—ecological and systems theory, strengths‑based practice, psychodynamic and cognitive‑behavioral theories, and critical and anti‑oppressive approaches. These inform diverse methods of intervention: micro‑level (casework, counseling), mezzo‑level (group work, family therapy), and macro‑level (community organizing, policy advocacy). The professional process follows a cyclical model: engagement → assessment → planning → intervention → evaluation → termination or revision. These activities take place across key domains of practice (child welfare, mental health, healthcare, schools, criminal justice, community development). The resulting outcomes include individual and family functioning, social justice and equity, community capacity, and policy reform.
